title: Congress opts out of Baramati byelection after ‘emotional appeals’
content: Decision comes after requests from NCP, NCP (SP) and Chief Minister Fadnavis; Maharashtra Congress chief says its decision to exit the polls does not mean that it supports the candidature of Mahayuti’s Sunetra Pawar

title: Sharan Prakash Patil takes umbrage at remarks by BJP leaders against Mallikarjun Kharge
content: The Minister claims that the Congress refrains from indulging in hate speech but welcomes criticism of its work and not personal attacks

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Assaults and confrontations shadow voting in Kottayam, Pathanamthitta
content: Poonjar legislator Sebastian Kulathunkal had a heated confrontation with the police near Koovappally in Kottayam  over the establishment of an LDF booth committee office and  installation of flex boards

title: Assam election 2026 LIVE: Polling in all 126 Assembly constituencies ends with estimated 84.42% voter turnout
content: Dalgaon recorded the highest voting at 94.57%, while the lowest turnout was in Amri at 70.40%, ECI said.

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: Over 86% voter turnout in Puducherry; 84% in Assam till 5 p.m.; 77.45% estimated polling in Kerala
content: Polling concludes in Kerala, Assam and Puducherry; Dharmanagar bypoll in Tripura records 79.84% turnout

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Commotion at Thrissur booth as woman with bandaged index finger denied permission to vote
content: Following hours-long standoff and protests, she was allowed to vote post 6 p.m. after submitting an affidavit

title: State committed to ensuring welfare of farmers, says Priyank Kharge
content: Our govt. has extended substantial financial support to the agricultural sector, according to the Minister

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: K. Sudhakaran rules out LDF continuity, predicts UDF win
content: He refutes reports suggesting that he worked against UDF candidate in Peravur and KPCC president Sunny Joseph

title: When historic markers in Chennai are demolished without explanation
content: The descendants of Dr. M.R. Gurusamy Mudaliar are asking as to why his name has been removed from a block at the Kilpauk Medical College (KMC) when the structure was rebuilt recently

title: Karnataka 2nd PUC exam-1 results: State records “historic” 86.48% pass percentage
content: Of the 6,48,853 students who registered, a total of 5,46,698 students passed the exam across Science, Commerce and Arts streams.

title: CBI opposes plea for judge’s recusal in Delhi liquor policy case
content: Agency tells Delhi High Court that attending seminar by Akhil Bharatiya Adhivakta Parishad cannot be ground for recusal sought by Arvind Kejriwal in liquor policy case

title: Venkaiah Naidu advocates girls’ education to drive social change
content: Addressing the annual convocation of Smt. N.P. Savithramma Government Women’s Degree College (Autonomous), the former Vice-President highlights that empowerment cannot be achieved through education alone

title: CPI(M) flags ‘mass disenfranchisement’ in West Bengal, urges EC to safeguard voting rights
content: In a letter to Chief Election Commissioner, CPI(M) General Secretary M.A. Baby alleges that the Special Intensive Revision led to the deletion of over 90 lakh voters from the electoral rolls in West Bengal

title: A.P. Speaker Ayyanna Patrudu seeks 33% reservation in legislatures for youth under 40
content: Andhra Pradesh Assembly Speaker calls for 33% reservation for those below 40 in legislatures at CPA West Zone meeting in Goa

title: Kerala election 2026 LIVE updates: As polling concludes, estimated voter turnout crosses 77%
content: The latest figures are higher than that of the 2021 Assembly polls which saw a polling percentage of 76%

title: Six-foot python survives after surgery in veterinary college in Tirunelveli
content: The six-foot snake, weighing around 4 kg, was brought for treatment by Moolakal forest ranger Balasubramanian with the assistance of the Karu Animal Rescue Unit Trust, Veeravanallur

title: High Commissioner Vikram Doraiswami awarded Freedom of the City of London
content: Vikram Doraiswami described his time in London as a “transformative period” for the bilateral relationship, including the conclusion of the India-U.K. Free Trade Agreement (FTA)

title: Karnataka bypolls at 5 pm: Bagalkot records 65.68% polling; voter turnout at 63.04% in Davangere South
content: The polls were necessitated in Bagalkot and Davangere South by the deaths of senior Congress MLAs H.Y. Meti and Shamanur Shivashankarappa respectively

title: Assembly of tunnel boring machine for Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project begins
content: National High Speed Rail Corporation Limited begins assembly of Tunnel Boring Machines for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train Project, including a seven km undersea tunnel beneath Thane Creek

title: Summer heat and oil shock raise concerns for India’s energy demand
content: Peak summer electricity demand, high crude oil prices, and West Asia tensions threaten to push India’s energy demand, import bill and inflation higher

title: ‘All art forms, including theatre, evolving with technology’
content: Kannada University Vice-Chancellor D.V. Paramashivamurthy was speaking at a programme at Pampa Sabhangan in Hampi

title: Nagaland Assembly bypoll: Voter turnout 80.62% till 5 p.m.
content: The polling process remained largely peaceful, officials said, adding that 18,045 out of 22,382 electors cast their votes

title: Nitish leaves for Delhi, may resign as Bihar CM after taking oath as Rajya Sabha member
content: The BJP may name its first Chief Minister in Bihar after April 14, and the new government may take oath at a function in the Raj Bhawan on April 15 or 16, a BJP leader said

title: Priyank Kharge dares RSS to file complaint on its letterhead, hits out at BJP leaders
content: If the RSS is a registered organisation, let it file a complaint on its own letterhead. Why does it seek others’ help? I too am facing cases and we are not deterred by such actions: Priyank

title: T.N. election: AIADMK’s promise of crop loan waiver raises questions of feasibility
content: The AIADMK’s scheme covers crop loans obtained by farmers from primary agricultural credit cooperative societies (PACCS) and loans obtained by persons with disabilities and pavement vendors from cooperative banks

title: Panel flags inconsistent accounting practices in health regulatory bodies
content: Committee says there has been an increase in revenue generated by regulatory bodies through application, inspection and licensing fees following the revision of norms

title: NMC asks medical colleges to restrict MBBS fees to 4.5-year academic period
content: National Medical Commission says charging fees for full five or five-and-a-half years of the MBBS course violates provisions of the NMC Act, 2019

title: Watch: Crossing the line: How Jewar residents risk their lives to catch a bus
content: As the Noida International Airport takes shape in Jewar, billions are being poured into infrastructure that promises to transform the region. But for the villages that surround the project, a basic need remains unmet - a safe place to catch a bus.

title: TCS Q4 net profit jumps 12.22% to ₹13,718 crore as margins expand, headcount rises by over 2,000
content: TCS reported a net profit of ₹12,224 crore in the January-March period year ago, and ₹10,657 crore in the preceding December quarter

title: Player: 'Heart goes out' for Tiger, but stop driving
content: Former Masters champion Gary Player said he sympathizes with Tiger Woods' ongoing pain from multiple injuries but says the 15-time major champion needs to hire a driver and stop operating vehicles while medicated.

title: OKC locks up No. 1 seed, joins elite company
content: The Thunder clinched the NBA's best regular-season record with a win over the Clippers to join some exclusive company.

title: Bryson bringing 3D-printed 5-iron to Masters
content: Bryson DeChambeau confirmed to ESPN that he'll play the Masters with a 5-iron that he fabricated himself with a 3D printer.

title: Cousins: I shouldn't play if not Raiders' best QB
content: Raiders quarterback Kirk Cousins is aware that Las Vegas is expected to draft Fernando Mendoza and says he only wants to play if it's determined he's the best option.

title: Study: MLB average salary hits record $5.34M
content: Major League Baseball's average salary rose 3.4% on Opening Day to a record $5.34 million, according to a study by The Associated Press, and the New York Mets topped spending at the season's start for the fourth straight year.

title: Reports: NCAA to discuss 5-year eligibility model
content: An NCAA panel is scheduled to discuss changes to eligibility rules that would incorporate age into the process, sources said Wednesday.

title: Prized recruit Hiller, No. 2 OT in '27, picks Florida
content: Maxwell Hiller, the No. 2 offensive tackle and No. 7 overall recruit in the SC Next Junior 300, gave Florida and new head coach Jon Sumrall his verbal pledge Wednesday.

title: Full of thanks, UCLA celebrates title at Pauley
content: Lauren Betts and her UCLA teammates celebrated the Bruins' first NCAA women's basketball championship with their fans at Pauley Pavilion on Wednesday night.

title: 📈 Ranking top men's hoops players in the portal
content: More than 1,000 players have entered the portal less than 24 hours after it opened. Here's the top talent.

title: The latest NFL draft intel for all 32 teams: What we're hearing on how the picks could play out
content: Our NFL reporters answer questions for every team two weeks out from the draft, and our analysts weigh in with some new intel on picks and moves.

title: Our draft experts picked 10-man rosters from the 2026 class: Who has the best superteam?
content: Mel Kiper Jr., Matt Miller, Jordan Reid and Field Yates drafted rosters of 2026 prospects. See their selections.

title: MLB Power Rankings: NL Central teams rise as AL powerhouse flails
content: Teams with championship hopes are falling down our list while a number of surprising starts have boosted the status of other unexpected contenders in Week 2.

title: NHL playoff watch: Clinching scenarios on 14-game Thursday
content: The playoff picture is coming into focus day by day. Here's the latest snapshot, plus projections for every team.

title: Hage, Verhoeff, Pohlkamp, more: Top NHL prospects playing in the Frozen Four
content: Scouting eyes from across the league will be fixed on the action in Las Vegas this weekend. Here are the top players to watch.

title: NC State men recount trainer's alleged sexual abuse
content: Lawsuit plaintiffs said they sought help but were subjected to unwanted genital touching or other misconduct.

title: What the Raiders can learn from last time they paired a first-time head coach with a No. 1 overall pick QB
content: The Raiders last paired a new coach with a rookie QB in 2007. Will it be different with Klint Kubiak and Fernando Mendoza?

title: '50 wins in a gap year': How Jaylen Brown turbocharged the Celtics back to title contention
content: Between memorizing the astrological signs of his teammates and undergoing extreme underwater training, Brown didn't need to be a hero in Boston. He simply needed to lead. "I'm not going to make myself small ever again."

title: NBA playoff watch: Celtics can clinch 2-seed
content: Boston visiting New York is Thursday's  top game. Plus, updated playoff projections and draft lottery standings.

title: Scottie Scheffler brings different vibes to Augusta than other star golfers
content: Scheffler might not generate the same buzz as players like Tiger and Phil, but he's a different kind of superstar, and maybe that's a good thing.

title: Free agency: Predictions, biggest questions, more
content: Who are the biggest names who might be on the move, and how will the condensed offseason impact free agency?

title: UFC 327 expert picks: How can Procházka reclaim the light heavyweight title?
content: Can Carlos Ulberg upset Jiří Procházka? Three MMA experts pick Saturday's UFC light heavyweight title fight.

title: Drones, hype videos and data: The number crunchers behind the USMNT's World Cup prep
content: Players decide games and coaches help to guide them, but U.S. Soccer has a team of number crunchers sifting through reams of data to give them an edge.

title: Maguire's remarkable Man United turnaround: 'It's made me stronger'
content: Fresh from signing a new deal at Manchester United, Harry Maguire opens up on some of the tougher times of his career at Old Trafford.

title: Connelly's favorite transfer classes: Teams that pushed the envelope in the portal
content: Looking beyond the numbers, here are the teams that took the boldest steps in their portal approaches.

content: Dick Vitale hands out superlatives for a season that had everything, from Diaper Dandies to Prime-Time Performers.

content: Dive into how women's professional wrestling has evolved on WWE's biggest stage over the past 40 years.

title: Six things I wish I'd known when I drafted
content: Tristan H. Cockcroft takes a look at biggest fantasy surprises from the first few weeks of the season.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live: Ceasefire Violations Carry Costs, Strong Responses, Says Iran Speaker
content: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates:  US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-day ceasefire just hours before the deadline he had set for Iran to capitulate, warning that otherwise a "whole civilisation will die tonight."

title: "Frustrated" US Demands Plans From Allies To Secure Hormuz In Days: Report
content: Trump has repeatedly called NATO a "paper tiger" and threatened to withdraw from the 32-member transatlantic alliance in recent weeks, arguing that Washington's European allies have relied on US security guarantees while providing inadequate support

title: PM Modi Slams Mamata Banerjee Over "Misguided" Fish Farming Policies, She Replies
content: West Bengal election 2026: "Despite the immense demand for fish here in Bengal, the state remains far from self-reliant in fish production," Prime Minister Narendra Modi said

title: Netanyahu's Corruption Trial To Resume On Sunday As Iran Attacks Halt
content: Netanyahu, the first sitting Israeli prime minister to be charged with a crime, denies charges of bribery, fraud and breach of trust brought in 2019.

title: 2 Bangladesh Cops Sentenced To Death For 2024 Student Protester Killing
content: Death sentences were handed down to two former low-level policemen, Amir Hossain and Sujan Chandra Roy, who are both in court to hear the verdict.

title: Indian Delegation To Visit Washington This Month To Advance US Trade Deal
content: This diplomatic movement coincides with Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri's three-day official visit to the United States.

title: UP Bureaucrat Sent Back After Failing To Enumerate Poll Booths In Bengal's Cooch Behar
content: Anurag Yadav was appointed the observer for Cooch Behar South on March 16 and he joined two days later. But yesterday, at a meeting with Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, he failed to answer questions.

title: Man Beheads Driver In Bihar Market, Dies After Being Thrashed By Locals
content: Meanwhile, images of Hussain's severed head, lying on the street, apparently captured by onlookers on their phones, have gone viral on social media.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Real Reason China Stayed 'Quiet' About Its US-Iran Ceasefire Role
content: Given Trump's ego and the need to declare 'victory', Beijing opted for a very quiet entry into the mediation, with Pakistan apparently fronting the whole exercise.

title: 'Danger Zones': Iran Shares Map Of Sea Mines In Hormuz Amid Ceasefire
content: The charts were dated from Feb 28 until Thursday, April 9, and it was unclear if the Guard had cleared any mining on the route since then.

title: 11,000 Litres Of Milk Poured In Narmada River, Sparks Faith vs Logic Debate
content: A viral video focuses on one particular ritual where nearly 11,000 litres of milk were poured into the Narmada as part of a ceremonial offering.

title: Message To Society: Kerala Couples Vote Before Tying The Knot
content: Joining them in the spirit of the day, another bride from northern Wayanad district also made sure to vote first before heading off to her own wedding ceremony.

title: 'Won't Take Eyes Off Putin': UK, Norway Led Op To Deter Russian Submarines
content: Asserting that Russia is the main threat to the UK and its allies, UK Defence Secretary John Healey said, "Putin would want us to be distracted by the Middle East."

title: At Rs 6,300 Crore, Delhi Records Over 10% Rise In Liquor Revenue
content: The revenue grew steadily throughout the year. By January 31, the Excise Department had already collected Rs 4,992.69 crore, government data shows.

title: Married At 14, Maharashtra Girl Dies By Suicide. Husband Arrested
content: The girl was subjected to mental and physical harassment and took the step after being driven to desperation by the abuse.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India Sits Atop 500 Million Tons Of Gold Ore. Why Isn't It Being Mined? - By Shashi Tharoor
content: We mine a pittance - barely one and a half tons a year - while draining our foreign exchange reserves to import hundreds of tons annually from mines in Australia, Ghana, and Peru.

title: "No BJP Factor": Shashi Tharoor To NDTV As Kerala Goes To Polls
content: Having travelled extensively during the campaign, Shashi Tharoor said the momentum on the ground points clearly towards a change in government.

title: Shock 40-day blackout: China seals off skies twice the size of Taiwan without warning
content: China has imposed a 40-day ban on civil aviation over a vast airspace near Shanghai, sparking speculation about military activities. The extensive, unexplained restriction, covering over 73,000 sq km, is unusual in its scale and duration, with experts suggesting it's for military exercises. This move is seen as part of China's broader strategy to assert influence in the Indo-Pacific.

title: Working abroad? How to stay compliant with India's tax residency rules - explained
content: India determines an individual’s tax residency primarily on the basis of physical presence (irrespective of the purpose of stay) within the country.

title: Infosys, HCL and other IT giants have filed multiple 'warn' notices in Texas: What it means
content: Indian IT giants Hinduja Global Services, Infosys, and HCL Technologies have filed numerous WARN notices in early 2026, exceeding 2025's total. This surge, driven by AI's impact on large deals and shrinking demand for redeployment, signals accelerating restructuring and potential workforce reductions.

title: PAN Card application 2026: Four new forms introduced - which is the right one for you?
content: These forms - Form 93, Form 94, Form 95, and Form 96, have been introduced to suit different categories of applicants, including Indian residents, foreign individuals, as well as domestic and overseas entities.

title: 'Cricket doesn't exist': Ex-B'desh cricketer invites 'all to witness the circus'
content: Bangladesh cricket is facing chaos, with off-field controversies overshadowing the sport. Aftab Ahmed criticised mismanagement, political influence, and media hype, calling it a “circus.” The cricket board’s dissolution and a politically linked new committee added to concerns. He mocked the situation, saying it’s more spectacle than sport, adding: “Cricket doesn’t exist anymore; the circus does.”

title: Did ChatGPT come first or Gemini: Google CEO Sundar Pichai answers
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai stated the company did not miss the AI chatbot wave but deliberately delayed its release. He revealed Google had a functional version of ChatGPT internally, but it was deemed too toxic and unrefined for public release, prioritizing product quality over being first to market.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 21: Ranveer Singh starrer dips under Rs 10 crore
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking spree, crossing Rs 1,000 crore net in India and achieving a worldwide gross of Rs 1,653.67 crore. Despite a slight dip on its 21st day, the film has secured a spot in the top 10 highest-grossing films globally for 2026, marking a significant international triumph for Indian cinema.

title: 'Never added fuel to fire': China rejects claims of military support to Iran
content: China has denied reports of supplying satellite imagery and chip manufacturing equipment to Iran. Beijing stated it has made its own efforts to push for a ceasefire in the region. This comes after Iran and the US agreed to a pause in hostilities. China maintains an objective and impartial stance on the Iran issue, promoting peace talks.

title: No trace of Pawan Khera; Banjara Hills home barricaded
content: Pawan Khera remains untraceable as Assam Police search for him in Hyderabad regarding alleged defamatory remarks against Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife. Khera's Hyderabad residence is under heavy police watch, while he has moved the Telangana High Court seeking anticipatory bail.

title: Oracle layoffs that shocked employees with 6 am email start to appear in public filings
content: Software giant Oracle is reportedly laying off around 700 workers in California, with job cuts expected by June 1. This move is part of a larger wave of layoffs, potentially impacting thousands, as the company invests heavily in AI infrastructure.  Meanwhile, Oracle faces scrutiny over its H-1B visa filings, sparking debate about American job displacement.

title: Narine comes back as KKR asked to bat first against LSG - check full playing XI
content: KKR got a big boost as ace spinner Sunil Narine came back into the side after missing last match against PBKS.

title: Court asks Aditya Dhar, Trimurti Films to mediate Oye Oye song dispute
content: Trimurti Films has filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against Aditya Dhar's production house, Rang De Lal (Oye Oye), over the alleged unauthorized use of the song 'Tirchi Topiwala' from 'Tridev' in 'Dhurandhar 2'. The Delhi High Court has ordered mediation to resolve the dispute, which appears largely financial, concerning royalty and compensation.

title: 'Miller mad?': Jofra Archer's 13-year-old post goes viral after GT beat DC
content: An old post by Jofra Archer went viral after David Miller’s unbeaten 41 nearly won a tense IPL 2026 match. He brought Delhi close but refused a crucial single, leading to a final-ball run-out by Jos Buttler off Prasidh Krishna. Archer’s tweet, “Miller mad? Cricket at 11:30,” perfectly matched the late-night drama.

title: 'Tell cops we are friends': B'luru doc's msg to woman after killing wife
content: A Bengaluru court denied bail to Dr. Mahendra Reddy GS, arrested for allegedly murdering his wife, Kruthika Reddy, by administering an overdose of anaesthesia. Police recovered digital evidence, including incriminating messages, suggesting Reddy planned the murder due to marital discord and his wife's ill health.

title: Watch: China unveils killer canine bots that share brain, primed for urban combat
content: China's military is revolutionizing warfare with advanced canine robots operating in coordinated "wolf packs." These units share intelligence and execute complex urban missions, a significant leap from earlier models. Specialized roles like reconnaissance and offensive operations are assigned, with integration for air-ground missions. This development signals a major evolution in military operations.

title: 'I want this kid out': Yuvraj reveals he asked Bumrah to leave the nets
content: Yuvraj Singh, in an interview, recalled asking a 16-year-old Jasprit Bumrah to stop bowling to him during nets due to his extreme pace.  Yuvraj, then making a comeback after cancer, found the young Bumrah's speed and deceptive bounce incredibly challenging.  He lauded Bumrah as "the best," highlighting his consistent, match-winning performances across all formats.

title: ‘Bomb-making became cottage industry’: PM Modi attacks Mamata Banerjee, TMC at Birbhum rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi fiercely criticized West Bengal's TMC government, accusing it of normalizing violence and turning crude bomb-making into a cottage industry. He promised strict action against infiltrators and land grabbers if the BJP forms the government, highlighting the Bogtui massacre as a blot on humanity and questioning the TMC's respect for tribal communities.

title: Trade deal back in focus: Indian delegation to visit US, says Envoy
content: The United States is set to host an Indian delegation this month, signaling renewed momentum in bilateral trade talks. This follows a productive meeting between US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is in the US to review trade, defense, and technology ties.

title: 'Riding a fireball': Artemis 2 crew braces for Orion's fiery re-entry
content: Artemis II's crew is preparing for a high-speed re-entry into Earth's atmosphere, a critical and dangerous phase of their mission. The Orion spacecraft's heat shield, which experienced issues on the uncrewed Artemis I mission, is under close watch as it faces extreme temperatures and pressure during its descent.

title: Bengal polls: At Bangladesh border, a fight for next chapter
content: Baharampur's political landscape, shaped by its historical legacy, is witnessing a fierce triangular contest. Once a Congress stronghold, the constituency saw a BJP breakthrough in 2021, followed by a TMC victory in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls. Religious polarization and security concerns are now key drivers, making the upcoming assembly election unpredictable.

title: Nashik: 8 IT company employees allege sexual harassment, 6 held
content: Nashik police have arrested six IT firm employees, including one accused of sexual exploitation under a false marriage promise. Five others face charges of sexual harassment and religious insult against women colleagues over four years. A special investigation team is probing nine registered cases, with an HR official also named as a suspect.

title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill punished despite GT’s last-ball thriller win over DC
content: Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans clinched a thrilling one-run victory against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Despite the dramatic win, Gill was fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate, marking the team's first offense. The captain's tactical decisions, including a crucial gamble on the final ball, ultimately secured the hard-fought win.

title: Rashmika Mandanna's father gifts her a bungalow on her 30th birthday
content: Actress Rashmika Mandanna received a heartwarming surprise on her 30th birthday from her father, who gifted her a bungalow named 'Serenity' near her hometown. This special occasion followed her recent wedding to Vijay Deverakonda. The actress also celebrated her first birthday post-marriage with a traditional Kodava ceremony in Virajpet, marking a significant new chapter.

title: Not 'chasemaster' Virat Kohli! David Miller, KL Rahul lead this IPL record
content: While RCB star Virat Kohli dominates IPL chase runs, he surprisingly doesn't top the average charts. David Miller leads this elite list with an impressive 49.08 average, followed by KL Rahul and Phil Salt.  Kohli sits tenth, highlighting that sheer volume isn't the only measure of chase prowess in T20 cricket.

title: Hezbollah chief’s nephew Ali Yusuf Harshi killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
content: Israel announced it killed Ali Yusuf Harshi, nephew of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem, in a Beirut strike. This occurred despite a ceasefire announcement in a separate conflict. Israeli forces targeted over 100 Hezbollah sites in Beirut and other areas. The strikes followed deadly attacks on commercial and residential areas in Beirut.

title: TCS Q4 FY26 results: 12% rise in net profit; dividend declared
content: For the financial year ended March 31, 2026, TCS said the final dividend will be paid on the third day after the conclusion of its 31st Annual General Meeting.

title: Sensex tanks 900 points: Top reasons for stock market crash
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Thursday, a day after the stock market saw a record single day gain. While Nifty50 went below 23,800, BSE Sensex was down over 1,000 points intraday.

title: Congress withdraws from Baramati bypoll, 22 independents deny Sunetra Pawar a walkover
content: The Congress has withdrawn its candidate from the Baramati by-election, following an advisory from Sharad Pawar to support NCP candidate Sunetra Pawar. Despite the Congress exit, over 30 independent candidates remain, prompting NCP efforts to persuade them to withdraw before the deadline.

title: ‘Pot of sins now full’: PM Modi attacks TMC’s ‘Nirmam sarkar’ at Asansol rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that a political change in West Bengal is imminent, with citizens demanding it. Addressing a rally in Asansol, he accused the ruling Trinamool Congress of fostering a 'syndicate raj' controlling the coal and sand sectors, vowing a BJP government would restore industrial strength and end mafia rule.

title: Was Lebanon part of ceasefire deal or not? How Pakistan may have muddled US-Iran truce
content: A ceasefire between the US and Iran is shrouded in confusion, with sharp disagreements over its scope, particularly regarding Lebanon. While the US insists the truce is limited to Iran, Pakistan and Iran suggest a broader agreement. This ambiguity, coupled with continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon and differing US negotiation frameworks, threatens the fragile peace ahead of crucial talks.

title: ‘Intent can’t be faulted, but…’: Gavaskar defends Miller after DC’s one-run heartbreak
content: Sunil Gavaskar defended David Miller's intent in Delhi Capitals' narrow IPL loss, emphasising execution challenges in high-pressure moments. He highlighted Ravi Shastri's 1986 tied Test heroics as an example of crucial game awareness. Meanwhile, Rashid Khan's stellar bowling secured Gujarat Titans' first win, though Kevin Pietersen noted overall bowling concerns.

title: Sabarimala case: Centre backs curbs on women’s entry, says 2018 verdict assumed 'men superior'
content: The Centre has told the Supreme Court that the Sabarimala temple's ban on women of menstruating age should be upheld, arguing the 2018 verdict was flawed. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta contended that religious practices aren't solely about gender and cited diverse traditions. The court is examining how equality principles intersect with religious freedom.

title: Ceasefire at risk: Iran closes Hormuz as Israel pounds Lebanon, Hezbollah fires back
content: A fragile Iran-US ceasefire is showing signs of collapse as Iran warns of renewed hostilities amid intensified Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Hezbollah retaliated, accusing Israel of breaching the truce, a stance Israel and the US argue falls outside the agreement's scope, jeopardizing upcoming peace talks.

title: 'Ships continuously coming even amid blockage': Centre assures 100% energy supplies
content: India's LPG supply remains unaffected by West Asian geopolitical tensions, with shipments continuing to arrive through the Strait of Hormuz without disruption. Domestic availability is stable at 100 percent, and the government is closely monitoring developments to ensure energy security and diversification.

title: Securing supply: India presses Iran to expedite movement of oil shipments through Hormuz
content: Despite the temporary pause in hostilities, industry executives have cautioned that a full return to normal oil trade could take at least three months.

title: 'Maine bahut matches finish kiye hai': Kohli comparison agitates Babar
content: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam sharply dismissed a journalist's comparison to Virat Kohli regarding match-finishing abilities. Despite not finishing the recent PSL victory himself, Azam asserted he has a strong record of closing out games, suggesting the journalist's perception is a misconception.

title: The good, the bad and the ugly of F1's new 2026 regulations
content: F1's new-for-2026 regulations have come in for considerable criticism, but there have been positives, too. ESPN weighs up the good and the bad.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool, Real Madrid work on mega midfield swap deal
content: Real Madrid and Liverpool are working on deals for Eduardo Camavinga and Alexis Mac Allister to swap clubs. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Rankings: Profiling more than 60 wide receivers
content: Mike Clay is revealing his fantasy football rookie rankings at each key offensive position . the rankings do not include rookies because we are unsure of their landing spots . all references to player ages are as of the season opener on sept. 9, 2026 .

title: Artemis II success sharpens focus on China’s 2030 crewed moon landing

title: Masters tiers: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .

title: Coaches, GMs weigh in on SEC's top newcomers

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal opened Monday with a tidal wave of activity . more than 1,300 players are looking for new homes . the portal closes April 20 but players' decisions for their next school can still come after that .

title: Sights and sounds from the first round of the Masters
content: Bubba Watson addresses Tiger Woods' DUI charge ahead of The Masters. (0:47) The 90th edition of the Masters Tournament began on Thursday, kicking off the first golf major of the year. It is one of the most iconic events in sports, and there was, as always, no shortage of iconic scenery at Augusta National as the tournament began. From the course to the concessions, all the classic staples were there to accompany the on-course play. Here are all the top sights and sounds from the first round of the Masters. Tom Watson, Jack Nicklaus and Gary Player serve as this year's Honorary Starters.  #themasters   pic.twitter.com/GZsT9TPtKT
